Healthy.issue: The mint leaves are healthy with no visible signs of plant disease or pest damage.
Healthy.treatment: Continue regular monitoring, maintain appropriate irrigation schedules, and ensure adequate exposure to sunlight.

Damaged.issue: Visible signs of physical damage, leaf tearing, or environmental stress detected on the mint leaves.
Damaged.treatment: Prune heavily damaged leaves, check for localized issues, and stabilize watering and nutrient supply.

Rust.issue: Mint Rust fungal disease detected. Characterized by orange-brown, dusty pustules on the undersides of the leaves.
Rust.treatment: Immediately isolate the plant, remove and safely dispose of infected leaves, and apply a suitable organic or chemical fungicide.

Spots.issue: Leaf Spots or pest-induced spotting observed. Often caused by insect bites or fungal/bacterial spot pathogens.
Spots.treatment: Inspect carefully for insects (like aphids or spider mites), use neem oil or insecticidal soap, and avoid overhead watering to prevent spore spread.
